snippets.description: The original money-leak surface: 4 of 6 offer deal_type mechanics (buy_one_take_one, free_item, free_upgrade, bundle) share a single case-fallthrough into cheapestEligibleUnitPrice — the 'cheapest cart line goes free' branch. This function is BYTE-IDENTICAL across all 4 fix commits (35981fa..cceb66b) per the plan's DO-NOT-TOUCH constraint; the fix lives entirely in the resolver's dispatch, not here.

Final resolver shape: (a) permanent deny for buy_one_take_one/bundle (never coupon-redeemable, added P1b, untouched by P2); (b) configured-path dispatch for free_item/free_upgrade — null benefit_product_id still rejects (D4 permanent safety net), else checkFreeBenefit() gates then computeRewardDiscountCents/computeFreeUpgradeDiscountCents compute the amount, dual-clamped and rejected if <=0; (c) an explicit ALLOWLIST for percentage_discount/fixed_discount only, itself dual-clamped with a <=0 reject (this <=0 reject is the P2-cycle fix for a legacy value-less-discount coupon that would otherwise resolve ok and burn for zero benefit); any other/unknown mechanic falls to a final reject. Runs on BOTH preview (POST /coupons/apply) and placement (POST /orders) via the single shared resolver, so one code path closes both.

The two new pure money functions. computeFreeUpgradeDiscountCents: qualifying line = menuItemId === benefitProductId with >=1 size-type option carrying a POSITIVE priceDeltaCents; per-line waived = sum of positive size deltas; across multiple qualifying lines takes Math.min (one unit per redemption, never scaled by quantity); returns 0 (never negative) when absent/no-size-option/only-non-positive-deltas. checkFreeBenefit gates on not_in_cart, then for free_upgrade only, no_upgrade_to_waive when the computed waive is <=0 — this is what prevents a ₱0 upgrade from ever resolving as a burnable success. free_item deliberately reuses computeRewardDiscountCents VERBATIM (zero new function needed for that mechanic).

Zero-floor clamp at order placement: Math.max(0, Math.min(discountCents + couponDiscountCents, subtotalCents)) — the added Math.max(0, ...) floor is defense-in-depth against a corrupt/negative raw discount_value (only reachable by direct SQL now that admin Zod forbids a non-positive value), ensuring the stored total can never exceed the subtotal in the opposite direction either.

Server-side admin cross-validation, shared between create (.superRefine, base schema kept .partial()-able per VALIDATE Execute-Agent Instruction E1) and PATCH. free_item/free_upgrade REQUIRE benefitProductId; percentage_discount/fixed_discount REJECT a benefitProductId AND REQUIRE a positive discountValueCents (closes the admin-authored side of the ₱0-burn-via-NULL-discount hole). The PATCH handler loads the EXISTING row first and validates the MERGED (existing + patch) state — the partial-update bypass trap named in the plan's Risks table — but only when the patch actually touches offerType/benefitProductId/discountValueCents, so a deactivate-only or rename PATCH on a pre-existing legacy-invalid row is never blocked from proceeding.

Benefit-product integrity check (finding F5 from the P2-cycle adversarial review): the referenced product must exist, must be ACTIVE, and must NOT be a deal product (is_deal=true). A deal-product benefit would always 400 at placement via the pre-existing is_deal x couponCode guard, creating a preview/placement disagreement; an inactive product can never be added to a cart to satisfy redemption. Called on both POST create and PATCH update whenever benefitProductId is non-null.

Mint-side twin of the resolver's redeem-side denies (F6 from the P2-cycle adversarial review): refuses to issue codes against (a) a free_item/free_upgrade offer with benefit_product_id NULL, (b) a buy_one_take_one/bundle offer (never coupon-redeemable), (c) a percentage/fixed offer with a NULL or non-positive discount_value. All three checks run BEFORE any coupon row is written, matching the existing malformed-payload 400 contract — this closes the class of 'code exists, will always be rejected at redemption' issuance.

Benefit-product picker for the offer detail page. Lists only ACTIVE, non-deal products (products.filter((p) => p.isActive && !p.isDeal)) — mirrors assertBenefitProductExists server-side so the picker can never present a choice the server would 400 on. Save is disabled until a product is both selected and changed from the current value (draft !== offer.benefitProductId).
snippets.content:
const options = products.filter((p) => p.isActive && !p.isDeal);
...
<Button disabled={!draft || !dirty} isLoading={mutation.isPending} onClick={() => mutation.mutate({ id: offer.id, input: { benefitProductId: draft } })}>
  Save benefit product
</Button>

snippets.description: Client-side twin of the admin/coupons.ts mint-side benefit-product guard: blocked = needsBenefitProduct(offerType) && !benefitProductId disables the Generate button and shows 'Configure a benefit product first.' before the user can even attempt a request that the server would 400.

The only schema change across all 4 commits: additive nullable FK, NO ACTION on delete/update, no backfill, no destructive DDL. All P2/P3 work builds on this single column — no further migrations were added.
snippets.content:
ALTER TABLE "offers" ADD COLUMN "benefit_product_id" uuid;
ALTER TABLE "offers" ADD CONSTRAINT "offers_benefit_product_id_products_id_fk" FOREIGN KEY ("benefit_product_id") REFERENCES "public"."products"("id") ON DELETE no action ON UPDATE no action;
